title: End-to-end self-improvement loop iteration test on the fixed HBR test-packet corpus
goal: End-to-end integration test that runs one full self-improvement loop iteration against the fixed 30-item HBR test-packet corpus (MT-150), proving the entire D.2 chain composes: target choice -> editable surface snapshot -> sandbox run -> eval -> multi-metric floor -> Goodhart sentinel -> PromotionGate -> recorder. Test uses a fixture editable-surface proposal (small RetrievalPolicy.top_k change) and asserts the iteration completes through every stage with typed outputs.
expected_diff_shape: New integration test src/backend/handshake_core/tests/self_improve_e2e_iteration_tests.rs. Reuses fixture src/backend/handshake_core/tests/fixtures/hbr_test_packet_corpus/items.json from MT-150. Adds tests/fixtures/self_improve_e2e/baseline_eval_result.json for baseline EvalResult.
implementation_notes: Per refinement D.4 ('End-to-end test: one full self-improvement loop iteration against fixed HBR test-packet corpus'). Test flow: (1) load HBR test-packet corpus fixture (30 items) via MT-150 loader; (2) construct LoopCore + scheduler + sentinel + gate + recorder with mocks where appropriate (mock sandbox, mock validator runner returning deterministic PASS/FAIL per item, mock OperatorGate that auto-approves); (3) seed history with baseline EvalResult from fixture (dev_pass=0.5, holdout_pass=0.5, latency_p95=120ms, capsule_bytes=24000); (4) invoke scheduler.try_schedule_next() and assert Schedule decision; (5) invoke loop_core.run_one_iteration() with TargetPicker pre-configured to propose RetrievalPolicy.top_k change from 6 to 8 for ValidatorHbrTestPacket task type; (6) assert iteration walks every LoopStage in order (ChooseTarget -> IsolateEditableSurface -> RunInSandbox -> ExecuteEval -> AcceptReject -> RecordAsMemory -> Complete); (7) assert EditableSurfaceSnapshot captured the before/after policy values; (8) assert EvalResult populated for train/dev/holdout splits; (9) assert PromotionDecision is Approved (because trial dev_pass=0.6 > baseline 0.5 and other metrics don't regress); (10) assert SentinelDecision is Continue (only one iteration so far); (11) assert iteration recorded via CapsuleRecorder mock; (12) assert FR-EVT-CAPSULE-INJECTED + FR-EVT-PROMOTION-DECISION emitted exactly once. Run with cargo test --release; assert wall-clock <30s (NOT a hard gate; logged warning).
risk_if_missed: Without end-to-end loop coverage the D.2 surfaces may compose incorrectly at the seams; integration validator cannot verify the loop pipeline against AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS.
validator_focus: Test exercises every D.2 surface in one run; mocks are explicit; FR events validated; perf signal logged.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Test runs in CI without external dependencies; all I/O surfaces are mocked.
Every LoopStage transition asserted; cannot pass while skipping stages.
FR events asserted exactly once each; double-emission is a test failure.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Self-improve E2E iteration test (self_improve_e2e_iteration_tests.rs) ships and reports 7 tests passing per builder handoff proof. The test composes real LoopCore stage executors (execute_isolate_surface, execute_run_in_sandbox), uses CountingSandbox + mock evaluator + mock CapsuleRecorder + mock auto-approve gate, and asserts sandbox.run was invoked, FR-EVT-CAPSULE-INJECTED + FR-EVT-PROMOTION-DECISION emitted. CONCERN: the test composes the D.2 chain via individual stage helpers rather than a single loop_core.run_one_iteration() entry point — this leaves the loop-driver wiring (the top-level orchestrator that calls stages in order with the cap and gate enforcement) untested. The MT-156 'Scheduler is the ONLY caller of LoopCore::run_one_iteration' invariant is structural in the docs but not asserted by this E2E. Inherits the MT-154/MT-155 gate-bypass risks.
